title: Tour: Dictaphone dashboard
subtitle: Record or import audio into transcribed notes
description: Guided walkthrough of the Dictaphone dashboard — record audio in the browser or drop in audio/video files, each saved as a transcribed Voice Note, plus the Voice Notes list beside it.

steps.title: The Dictaphone
steps.body:
The **Dictaphone** turns speech into saved, searchable **Voice Notes** — record straight from your mic, or drop in an audio/video file and let it transcribe. Let's walk through it.

Use **Next** to step through, or **Esc** to leave anytime.

steps.title: The Dictaphone dashboard
steps.body: This dashboard pairs the **Dictaphone** recorder on the left with your **Voice Notes** list on the right, so new notes appear right next to where you make them.

steps.title: Language hint
steps.body: Leave this on **Auto-detect**, or pick a language to nudge the transcriber when you know what you'll be speaking. It applies to both recordings and imports.

steps.title: Record from your mic
steps.body: Press **Record** to start capturing from your microphone; the button becomes **Stop**. When you stop, you'll get a preview with an auto-filled, editable transcript and a title — then **Save**, **Re-record**, or **Discard**. (No need to record right now.)

steps.title: Or import a file
steps.body: Already have audio or video? **Drop files here** or use **Choose files** — each is converted, transcribed, and saved as a Voice Note automatically, with progress shown below.

steps.title: Your Voice Notes
steps.body: Every saved note lands in this **Voice Notes** list — title, audio, and the transcript stored together, so you can read and search them without playback. Click a row to open the full note.

steps.title: That's the Dictaphone
steps.body: Record or import on the left, and your transcribed notes collect on the right. For quick type-to-speak and push-to-talk, try the **Voice Studio** from the menu.

title: Tour: Voice Dictaphone tool
subtitle: Capture a voice note from anywhere
description: Guided walkthrough of opening the Voice Dictaphone as a standalone tool from the sidebar Tools section, then recording or importing audio into a transcribed Voice Note from its floating window.

steps.title: The Dictaphone, on demand
steps.body:
The **Voice Dictaphone** can be popped open as a **tool** from the menu — capture a quick voice note or drop in a file without navigating to its dashboard. Let me show you.

Use **Next** to step through, or **Esc** to leave anytime.

steps.title: Open the menu
steps.body: Tools live in the **menu**. Click any of the highlighted buttons on the left edge to open it — I'll continue as soon as it's open.

steps.title: Launch the Dictaphone
steps.body: Under the **Voice** app's **Tools** section, click **Voice Dictaphone**. (If the Voice app is collapsed, expand it first.) It opens as its own window.

steps.title: Here it is
steps.body: The **Dictaphone** opened as a floating window. Drag its header to move it or close it with the **✕** when you're done — anything you save lands in your Voice Notes either way.

steps.title: Record from your mic
steps.body: Press **Record** to start; the button becomes **Stop**. After stopping you'll get a preview with an editable, auto-filled transcript and a title — then **Save**, **Re-record**, or **Discard**. (No need to record right now.)

steps.title: Or import a file
steps.body: Prefer to import? **Drop audio or video files here** or use **Choose files** — each is converted, transcribed, and saved as a Voice Note automatically.

steps.title: That's the tool
steps.body: Open the **Voice Dictaphone** from the menu whenever a thought strikes, capture it, and close it — your transcribed notes are always waiting on the Dictaphone dashboard's Voice Notes list.

title: Tour: Voice Settings dashboard
subtitle: Choose the voice and playback used everywhere
description: Guided walkthrough of the Voice Settings dashboard — playback speed, the auto-speak toggle, and picking/previewing the default voice used when assistant messages are read aloud.

steps.title: Voice Settings
steps.body:
**Voice Settings** is where you choose the voice and playback options used whenever the platform reads assistant messages aloud. Let's take a look.

Use **Next** to step through, or **Esc** to leave anytime.

steps.title: The Voice Settings dashboard
steps.body: This is the **Voice Settings** dashboard. Everything here is saved to your profile and applies across apps that speak text aloud — let's walk through it.

steps.title: Playback speed
steps.body: Drag the **Speed** slider to set how fast spoken messages play, from 0.5× to 2×. The readout beside it shows your current setting.

steps.title: Auto-speak
steps.body: Turn this on to have **assistant messages read aloud automatically** by default — no need to press play each time.

steps.title: Choose your voice
steps.body: This is your **voice catalog**. Click any row to make it your selected voice — a check mark and the chip at the top show which one is active.

steps.title: Preview before you commit
steps.body: Use **Preview** on any voice to hear a short sample without changing your selection — press it again to stop. Try a few to find the one you like.

steps.title: All set
steps.body: Your voice and playback preferences are saved as you change them. Head to the **Studio** or **Dictaphone** from the menu to put your chosen voice to work.

title: Tour: Voice Settings tool
subtitle: Adjust your voice & playback from the menu
description: Guided walkthrough of opening Voice Settings as a standalone tool from the sidebar Tools section, then setting playback speed, auto-speak, and your default voice.

steps.title: Voice Settings, on demand
steps.body:
**Voice Settings** can be opened as a **tool** straight from the menu — a quick way to change your voice or playback without leaving your work. Let me show you.

Use **Next** to step through, or **Esc** to leave anytime.

steps.title: Open the menu
steps.body: Tools live in the **menu**. Click any of the highlighted buttons on the left edge to open it — I'll continue as soon as it's open.

steps.title: Launch Voice Settings
steps.body: Under the **Voice** app's **Tools** section, click **Voice Settings**. (If the Voice app is collapsed, expand it first.) It opens as its own window.

steps.title: Here it is
steps.body: **Voice Settings** opened as a floating window. Drag its header to move it or close it with the **✕** when you're done — your changes are saved to your profile as you make them.

steps.title: Playback speed & auto-speak
steps.body: Set the **Speed** spoken messages play at, and toggle **Auto-speak** beside it to have assistant replies read aloud automatically.

steps.title: Pick & preview a voice
steps.body: Click any voice in the list to select it, or use its **Preview** button to hear a sample first. The active voice is marked with a check and shown in the chip at the top.

steps.title: That's the tool
steps.body: Whenever you want to retune your voice or playback, summon **Voice Settings** from the menu in any app and close it when you're finished.

title: Tour: Voice Studio dashboard
subtitle: Type to speak, speak to transcribe
description: Guided walkthrough of the Voice Studio dashboard — the text-to-speech and speech-to-text workbench: engine health, the speak controls, and the push-to-talk transcriber.

steps.title: Welcome to Voice Studio
steps.body:
**Voice Studio** is your hands-on bench for the Voice app — type text and hear it spoken, or speak and watch it transcribe. Let's take a quick look.

Use **Next** to step through, or **Esc** to leave anytime.

steps.title: The Voice Studio dashboard
steps.body: This is the **Voice Studio** dashboard. Its panel holds the full text-to-speech and speech-to-text workbench — let's walk through it.

steps.title: Engine health at a glance
steps.body: These pills show whether the **TTS** (speak) and **STT** (listen) engines are online. Press **Refresh** anytime to re-check — if either reads *down*, playback or transcription won't work until it recovers.

steps.title: Type something to speak
steps.body: Whatever you type here is what the **Speak (TTS)** engine will read aloud. Try a sentence — nothing is sent until you press Play.

steps.title: Pick a voice & speed
steps.body: Choose which **voice** reads your text from the dropdown, and set the **Speed** beside it (0.5×–2×). The voices come from the app's cloneable voice catalog.

steps.title: Hear it
steps.body: **Play** synthesizes your text with the selected voice and plays it back. This same little speaker widget can be embedded anywhere in the platform to read text aloud.

steps.title: Speak to transcribe
steps.body: Now the other direction: **press and hold** this mic button while you talk, then release to transcribe. Results collect below, and the **→ TTS** shortcut drops a transcript straight back into the speak box.

steps.title: That's the Studio
steps.body: You've seen the whole loop — **type → speak**, and **speak → transcribe**. Explore the other Voice surfaces (the **Dictaphone** and **Voice Settings**) from the menu whenever you're ready.

title: Tour: Voice Studio tool
subtitle: Pop the Studio open from the menu, anywhere
description: Guided walkthrough of launching Voice Studio as a standalone tool from the sidebar Tools section, then using its speak and listen controls in the floating window.

steps.title: Voice Studio, on demand
steps.body:
**Voice Studio** isn't only a dashboard — it's a **tool** you can pop open from the menu in any app, as its own floating window. Let me show you how.

Use **Next** to step through, or **Esc** to leave anytime.

steps.title: Open the menu
steps.body: Tools live in the **menu**. Click any of the highlighted buttons on the left edge to open it — I'll continue as soon as it's open.

steps.title: Launch Voice Studio
steps.body: Under the **Voice** app's **Tools** section, click **Voice Studio**. (If the Voice app is collapsed, expand it first.) It opens as its own window.

steps.title: Here it is
steps.body: **Voice Studio** opened as a floating window. Drag its header to move it, **pin** it to a side edge to keep it handy, or close it with the **✕** when you're done — it behaves the same on any desktop.

steps.title: Speak (TTS)
steps.body: Type into the box above, choose a **voice** and **speed**, then press **Play** to synthesize and hear it. Quick text-to-speech without leaving whatever you were doing.

steps.title: Listen (STT)
steps.body: **Press and hold** the mic button to talk, release to transcribe. The transcript appears below, and **→ TTS** sends it back to the speak box — handy for round-tripping.

steps.title: That's the tool
steps.body: Because it's a standalone tool, you can summon **Voice Studio** from the menu in any app and close it the moment you're finished — no dashboard required.
